segments.text: Did you hit your own face with a hammer just to look like a model? Well, this 20-year-old did, and he's making over $100,000 a month because of it. Yep. His
name is Braden Peters, and he'll do literally anything to look attractive.
From drugs, steroids, surgeries to breaking his own bones. He's been doing it all since he was 15 because he believes when bones break, they grow
back stronger. Now, this obsession is part of a whole movement called looks maxing. And the culture runs on something called moging where the whole
point of being attractive is to make other men feel inferior. Now, at first glance, it all seems like harmless self-improvement. And to an extent, it
is, but researchers have come out and stated that all of this can be chased back to forums which are tied to real world violence, just rebranded as
self-improvement. So yeah, on this side of the internet, you've got 13 year olds uploading selfies to be rated by strangers on a genetic value scale, and
boys who score low get told that they are subhuman. Now, I know a lot of you engage with these memes and laugh at it.
But for a young kid on the internet looking for an identity, this can actually lead to scary places. In fact,
the Turing Institute states that nearly half of looks maxing videos on the internet are tagged with black pill hashtags, which means the algorithm
walks kids straight from jawline tips to content glorifying mass shooters and suicide. So before you engage and share these memes, please stop and think about
the second order effects of this content and share the story with your peers.
